content: Just as the English language definition of reflexion encompasses both literal light reflection and deliberate cognitive reflection on one’s own thinking, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be designed to incorporate two complementary modes: a fast, passive "reflection" mode that mirrors inconsistent solving outputs like a mirror bounces light, and a slow, deliberate "reflexion" mode that revisits reasoning to correct errors, combining Self-Contrast’s inconsistent perspective approach with structured iterative self-critique. This dual-mode design outperforms single-perspective reflection by capturing both implicit inconsistencies and explicit gaps in reasoning.
